---
title: "Welcome To My Blog"
author: "Ying Hui Tan"
date: "2026-05-31"
categories: [intro]
image: IMG_9731.jpeg
---

Hi everyone! The main purpose of this blog is to record the projects I did during my academic year and venture on interesting projects along the way. When I was choosing my major, I have no idea what Industrial and Systems Engineering (ISE) is all about and the course page does not really provide much information. I guess for now, I feel that ISE is a major that provides a lot of breadth in different areas of study.

I realised that Industrial and Systems Engineering consists of a lot of different components that many may not really understand, like Operation Research (OR), Design of Experiment (DoE), Discrete Event Simulation (DES), Time Series etc. I hope that through my blog, readers could understand what this major is truly about.

Some areas that I am particularly interested is DoE and some Machine Learning implementations. Hope that this blog can provide a better understanding of what ISE truly is for people who are interested in knowing what ISE is about.

For people who are geniunely interested in the blog, much appreciated if you could provide any forms of feedback on the analysis!

As for why the blog name is called "Confounding Human", I struggled with finding confounding factors in an Incomplete Block Design in a DoE module. Confounding is an interesting concept, just like how humans and experiments are; it is imperfect and always has unexpected outcomes.
